title: How to Send Transactional Emails in Laravel: A Complete Developer's Guide
author: Juan Rodriguez
excerpt: Master transactional email delivery in Laravel with queues, mailables, notifications, and production best practices for reliable sending at any scale.
content:
Transactional emails are the backbone of user communication in any Laravel application. From password resets to order confirmations, these critical messages must be delivered reliably and instantly. In this guide, we'll cover everything you need to know about how to send transactional emails in Laravel, from basic setup to production-scale delivery with queues and monitoring.

Transactional emails have 8x higher open rates than marketing emails—getting them right is essential for user trust and engagement.

Setting Up Laravel Mail Configuration

Before sending your first transactional email, configure your mail driver in config/mail.php. Laravel supports SMTP, Mailgun, Postmark, Amazon SES, and SendGrid out of the box. For production, use a dedicated transactional email provider or our self-hosted laravel transactional email platform that gives you full control over deliverability.

Creating Laravel Mailables

Laravel mailables are the recommended way to send transactional emails. Generate a mailable with php artisan make:mail OrderConfirmation, then define your email template using Blade or Laravel markdown mail. Mailables support attachments, inline images, and dynamic data binding for personalized transactional messages.

Using Laravel Notifications for Email

Laravel's notification system provides a unified API for sending laravel notification email across mail, database, SMS, and more. Define a notification class, implement the toMail() method, and send with a single line: $user->notify(new OrderShipped($order)). Perfect for transactional flows like welcome emails, payment confirmations, and account alerts.

Queueing Transactional Emails for Performance

Never send transactional emails synchronously in production. The laravel mail queue ensures your application stays responsive while emails process in the background. Configure the ShouldQueue interface on your mailables and notifications, then monitor laravel mail queue performance with Horizon or our built-in queue monitoring tools. For high-volume apps, this is essential for laravel send millions of emails without timeouts.

SMTP Driver

Configure laravel smtp with any provider. Simple setup for development and low-volume sending.

Mailgun Driver

Laravel mailgun integration for high-deliverability transactional email at scale.

Postmark Driver

Laravel postmark delivers sub-millisecond transactional emails with industry-leading speed.

Amazon SES

Laravel amazon ses for the lowest cost per email. Perfect for high-volume transactional sending.

Monitoring Deliverability with Email Tracking

After implementing your transactional email system, monitoring is critical. Laravel email tracking gives you visibility into opens, clicks, bounces, and complaints. Set up laravel email webhook endpoints to receive real-time delivery events. Combine with laravel email analytics to track deliverability trends and identify issues before they impact your users.

Best Practices for Production

When you deploy transactional email to production, follow these best practices: always use queues for reliability, implement bounce handling with webhooks, monitor laravel email queue performance, set up failover mail drivers, and test extensively with laravel mail testing tools. A robust laravel transactional email system is the foundation of user trust in any application.
